mapping.a_element: Kalman filter as a recursive optimization method minimizing quadratic error
mapping.b_element: Kalman filter as a recursive estimation method minimizing average quadratic error

mapping.a_element: state constraints (equality/inequality) as additional optimization conditions
mapping.b_element: heavy-tailed (Lévy) noise distributions as a generalization of Gaussian noise

mapping.a_element: nonlinear constraints requiring modified optimization approaches
mapping.b_element: nonlinear matrix equation arising from heavy-tailed noise distributions

brief_justification: While both papers extend the Kalman filter, Paper A focuses on incorporating state constraints into the optimization framework, whereas Paper B generalizes the noise model to heavy-tailed distributions, replacing the covariance matrix with a tail-covariance matrix. The core machinery differs: one modifies the optimization problem with constraints, the other redefines the statistical assumptions and resulting equations.
